summary: blind_scorer.py, blind_inputs.json, blind_expected_hidden.json, and deterministic hashes must stay separated.
Visible transcript and summaries replayed without lineage refs.
The visible summary retains that a blind pack exists but drops which files are visible inputs and which are hidden expected keys.
The visible path omits the transition from narrative scenario notes to key-only scorer inputs.
The replay assumes an external evaluator can infer expected keys from visible summaries.
The pack must hide expected outcomes, while a draft visible summary included exact expected reconstruction keys.
The scorer must avoid narrative claims, while the baseline draft pointed it at comparative prose.
The external plan excludes private substrate internals, while the draft dependency list required local Inside Voice internals.
The visible replay loses why hidden expected keys are excluded from the visible-only path.
The summary compresses the blind pack into 'scorer ready' and drops the no-narrative scoring constraint.
The recovery loop retries by removing narrative claims from scoring input, rerunning the scorer, and comparing reconstruction deltas.
Lineage refs, pressure rankings, and runtime hashes replayed.
Reconstruct the blind-pack boundary without exposing hidden expected outcomes to the visible-only replay path.
Probe statuses compared without narrative claim scoring.
